intro: Pleroma gertii, also known as Brazilian plumeria or yellow frangipani, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pleroma gertii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pleroma gertii is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of traditional remedies such as cough syrups and expectorants.
